whatThisIs: Show the numeric reference values from the current AHA CPR guidelines: compression rate (100-120 per minute for all ages), compression depth (at least 2 inches / 5 cm for adults, at least one-third the anterior-posterior chest diameter for children and infants - roughly 2 inches and 1.5 inches respectively), compression-to-ventilation ratios (30:2 for adults / single-rescuer pediatric, 15:2 for two-rescuer pediatric and neonates, 3:1 for neonatal resuscitation), ventilation rate with an advanced airway (1 breath every 6 seconds for adults, 1 every 2-3 seconds for pediatric), and the post-ROSC ventilation rate (10 per minute adult, 20-30 per minute pediatric).
whenToUse: Use this as a refresher card before a code, during a code-team debrief when reviewing whether the team hit AHA-recommended compression metrics, or when a learner is preparing for an AHA BLS / ACLS / PALS course. The card is reference data - actual CPR quality depends on coaching, feedback devices, and minimizing pauses; numbers alone do not produce ROSC.
